records.ety: [L. obsessus, p. p. of obsidere to besiege; ob (see Ob-) + sedere to sit.]

defs: To excessively preoccupy the thoughts or feelings of; to haunt the mind persistently.
To be excessively or persistently preoccupied with something; -- usually used with on or over; as, to obsess over an imagined insult.
quotes: At all ages children are driven to figure out what it takes to succeed among their peers and to give these strategies precedence over anything their parents foist on them. Weary parents know they are no match for a child's peers, and rightly obsess over the best neighborhood in which to bring their children up.
